model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Vanderbilt's elite offense, ranked 6th nationally in adjusted efficiency, provides a significant advantage against a Nebraska team that relies heavily on its 7th-ranked defense but struggles more on the offensive end. While the Cornhuskers' disciplined Big Ten style can stifle many opponents, the Commodores' SEC-tested athleticism and the return of scoring threat Duke Miles should allow them to find enough cracks in the defense to advance. Ultimately, Vanderbilt's superior overall efficiency margin and offensive firepower make them the more reliable pick to reach the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Elite Offense vs. Elite Defense
reasoningStep.summary: This matchup features a classic clash between Vanderbilt's top-10 offense and Nebraska's top-10 defense, with the Commodores holding a slight analytical edge.
evidence: Vanderbilt ranks 6th in AdjOE and 12th overall in KenPom.
Nebraska ranks 7th in AdjDE and 14th overall in KenPom.
Vanderbilt's offensive efficiency (127.3) significantly outpaces Nebraska's (118.6).
Vanderbilt guard Duke Miles is active after returning from a mid-season knee procedure.

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
